commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bfee8dac95992f6cb366e9983955f09354949ee0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Policy plugin conformance checks, docs, and tests for ingress DM/group posture plus related tool/sandbox posture and a new `plugin-sdk/tool-policy-conformance` subpath.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows `channels.defaults` only contributes `groupPolicy`, while the changed docs say defaults posture can satisfy ingress policy. The feature itself is additive, so there is no bug reproduction beyond that docs/code mismatch.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer judgment on the public SDK/config policy surface; this is not a safe automated repair lane while the proof gate is unmet.

target.title: Policy: add ingress channel conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds config-level ingress/channel policy conformance checks for DM scope, DM policy allowlists, open group denial, and mention-required group posture.
- Records ingress evidence only when an ingress policy section is present, so unrelated policy hashes and findings do not drift.
- Documents the offered policy syntax and the deliberately narrow boundary: this checks configured posture, not runtime sender authorization, pairing stores, live membership, or secret management.

Stack note: this is the next config-policy PR after sandbox posture conformance. It targets `main` as a normal draft PR because the intermediate stack branch is not an upstream base branch.

Policy syntax offered:

```jsonc
{
  "ingress": {
    "session": {
      "requireDmScope": "per-channel-peer"
    },
    "channels": {
      "allowDmPolicies": ["pairing", "allowlist", "disabled"],
      "denyOpenGroups": true,
      "requireMentionInGroups": true
    }
  }
}
```

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
  - 2 files passed, 173 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
  - All matched files use the correct format.
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
  - Docs MDX check passed.
- `git diff --check`
  - Clean.
- `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --base fork/policy-sandbox-posture-conformance`
  - No discrete, actionable correctness issues found after the final fixes.

Known proof gap:

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false` is currently blocked by pre-existing WhatsApp/Baileys type errors unrelated to this policy change.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` policy conformance can now report nonconforming configured ingress posture for session DM scope, configured channel DM policies, configured group openness, and configured group mention requirements.

Real environment tested: WSL-native OpenClaw checkout under `/root/src/openclaw-policy-ingress-conformance` on the `policy-ingress-channel-conformance` branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: Focused policy doctor tests cover allowed and denied DM scopes, DM policies, disabled channel/account skips, nested groups/topics, inherited supported group defaults, omitted `unspecified` posture, group-policy disabled suppression, and re-enabled nested group mention requirements.

Observed result after fix: 2 test files passed with 173 tests passing; formatting, docs MDX, and diff whitespace checks also passed.

What was not tested: Live provider ingress, pairing stores, live channel membership, sender authorization, and secret/config value inspection, because this PR intentionally implements config conformance only.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
